description: Compiler design, interpretation, and language implementation.

facts.context: Lexical analysis (tokenization) converts source code to:
distractors: machine code ready for direct execution by CPU
abstract syntax trees with full structure
intermediate representation for optimization

facts.context: LL vs LR parsers differ in that LL parsers:
distractors: are strictly more powerful than LR parsers
cannot handle any recursive grammar rules
require backtracking on every production

facts.context: SSA (Static Single Assignment) form requires each variable:
distractors: to be immutable with no reassignment ever allowed
stored in a register rather than memory always
having a unique name across all functions

facts.context: JIT compilation improves over interpretation by:
facts.truth: compiling hot paths to native code at runtime
distractors: eliminating the need for any runtime entirely
making startup time faster than AOT compilation
using less memory than interpreted execution

facts.context: Register allocation maps variables to registers using:
facts.truth: graph coloring or linear scan algorithms
distractors: random assignment with no optimization strategy
user annotations specifying register assignments
unlimited virtual registers with no spilling

facts.truth: code size for call overhead elimination
distractors: nothing since inlining is always beneficial
correctness for performance improvements
stack space for heap allocations
at runtime with caching for repeated use
never since all arithmetic is dynamic
only in debug builds for verification

facts.context: Dead code elimination removes code that:
facts.truth: has no effect on program output
distractors: contains bugs detected by static analysis
uses deprecated language features only
is commented out in the source file

facts.truth: replicating loop body to reduce iterations
distractors: converting all loops to recursive functions
parallelizing loops across multiple threads
eliminating loop bounds checks entirely
the final executable format for direct CPU execution
source code encoded in binary format only
debug symbols separate from program logic

facts.context: GC safepoints in compiled code are locations where:
facts.truth: thread state is consistent for collection
distractors: garbage collection is permanently disabled
the program automatically saves its state to disk
exceptions can be thrown and caught safely
faster linking with no additional optimization
dynamic linking at program startup only
separate compilation without whole-program view
